page: Google Cloud Platform x4-960-16t-metal is a bare-metal server instance belonging to the x4 server family. It features an x86_64 CPU architecture with 960 dedicated virtual CPUs and a massive system memory capacity of 16,384 GB. This configuration yields approximately 17.07 GB of RAM per vCPU core. The instance does not include local NVMe storage or integrated GPU hardware accelerators, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given its bare-metal deployment model, the server provides direct access to the underlying physical hardware, eliminating virtualization overhead. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes it highly efficient for workloads requiring massive in-memory datasets. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the extreme resource density of this hardware class is designed to consolidate massive enterprise workloads into a single physical footprint, reducing multi-node networking overhead. However, the lack of local storage means all storage operations must rely on network-attached storage volumes, which introduces network latency tradeoffs for disk-heavy operations.

This server configuration is optimized for large-scale enterprise applications. Suitable workloads include massive in-memory databases such as SAP HANA, large-scale data analytics, complex enterprise resource planning systems, and memory-intensive scientific simulations that require a unified memory space rather than distributed cluster nodes.
description: Google Cloud Platform x4-960-16t-metal is a bare-metal server instance in the x4 family, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 960 dedicated vCPUs and 16,384 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 17.07 GB per vCPU. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring network-attached storage for data persistence. By offering direct hardware access without virtualization overhead, this bare-metal configuration delivers high resource density and eliminates multi-node latency for massive datasets. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for consolidating large-scale workloads that demand a unified memory space. The server is optimized for massive in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and large-scale data analytics.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform x4-960-16t-metal is a bare-metal server featuring 960 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 16,384 GB of RAM, optimized for massive in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.

tagline: A bare-metal instance delivering massive memory capacity and dedicated compute cores for large-scale in-memory enterprise workloads.

No local storage, requiring network-attached storage
Optimized for massive in-memory databases and ERP systems
